signals.observation: Mother-in-law (esp. groom's mother) is the silent veto-holder on Chinese SG wedding gift conventions — she sets table count, ang bao expectations toward 'face' relatives, and whether ang bao goes to couple or to her as 'parents' contribution recovery'.
signals.evidence: SingaporeBrides forum threads 2024-2025 ('MIL wants ang bao to cover her invited guests' tables') and Reddit r/askSingapore 2025-08 thread '我妈说红包要给她' (~180 upvotes) document repeated cases where parents, not couple, are de facto recipients. SAYS 2024 piece on 'parents-paid-for-the-banquet' norm: ~40% of Chinese SG weddings are parent-funded in part, parents claim partial ang bao recovery rights.
signals.implication_for_us: Couple-side onboarding flow MUST surface a 'who's invited whom?' question and offer a split-allocation toggle (e.g. 'parents' guests → cash to parents; our guests → charity'). A single-pool 'all to charity' default will lose the 30% of couples whose parents funded part of the banquet. Add a 'family-elder FAQ' card explicitly addressing 'how do I tell my mum?' — this is the highest-frequency couple-side blocker.

signals.observation: Ang bao amount is socially visible at the receiving end — guests sign a register, and the couple/family count contents post-banquet. The 'ang bao box' near the entrance is performative; rate-shaming below the door rate is a real social risk.
signals.evidence: SingaporeBrides 'wedding ang bao etiquette' (updated 2025-09) describes the signature book + ang bao box ritual. Mothership SG 2024-11 piece on 'guests who underpay get talked about' cites multiple anonymous wedding-helper accounts of post-event ang bao tallying. Bridal Club SG 'wedding day timeline' explicitly schedules 'ang bao counting' for parents/siblings after dinner.
signals.implication_for_us: If guest scans Altru QR instead of dropping cash in the box, the couple's family loses the public counting ritual — and the guest loses face-saving anonymity within range of the rate. Altru must give the couple a real-time donation tally (private to couple) AND give guests rate guidance ('most guests at MBS give S$280-380') so nobody underpays by accident. Hide individual donor names from public surfaces by default; opt-in only.

signals.observation: Singapore scores HIGH on Hofstede long-term-orientation (72) and uncertainty-avoidance (8 — extremely low). Translated: SG culture tolerates novel rituals IF they're framed as forward-looking/legacy ('build something lasting') rather than as breaks with tradition.
signals.evidence: Hofstede Insights Singapore country profile (theculturefactor.com, refreshed 2024): LTO 72, UAI 8, IDV 20 (collectivist), PDI 74 (high power-distance), MAS 48 (balanced). LTO score is among Asia's highest — SG already privileges legacy/savings over short-term.
signals.implication_for_us: Hero copy should lead with legacy framing ('start your marriage with a legacy') over disruption framing ('break tradition'). High UAI tolerance means SG couples will TRY a new mechanic without needing decades of social proof — a key Altru tailwind vs HK/JP. But high power-distance (74) means parental endorsement matters: build a 'parents' page' that frames Altru as continuous with filial piety (your gift honours your parents' values), not rebellious.

signals.observation: SG-Chinese ang bao norms are downstream of HK/Mainland/Taiwan conventions but DIVERGE on cover-the-cost expectation. HK weddings: ang bao closer to symbolic (HKD 500-1,000 typical regardless of venue). Mainland: huge regional variance + 'gift wars'. Taiwan: NTD 2,200 / 2,600 / 3,600 fixed bands. SG uniquely pegs to venue cost.
signals.evidence: Tatler Asia HK Wedding Guide 2024 (HKD 500-1,200 hotel ang bao norm); Sixth Tone 2023 piece on Mainland China '禮金 inflation' (varies RMB 200-2,000+ regionally); Taiwan PTT wedding board norms 2024 (NTD 2,200 single, 3,600 with plus-one); Seedly 2024-2025 SG cover-the-plate guide. SG hotel ang bao S$280 ≈ USD 210 vs HK ≈ USD 90 — SG is the regional outlier.
signals.implication_for_us: Altru's cover-the-plate problem is uniquely SG-acute — the framing needs more care here than it would in HK or TW. Do NOT copy ang bao copy from HK wedding fintech; the cultural contract is different. For SG-HK or SG-TW expat couples, build a guest-side 'expected rate' hint by guest's home country to reduce confusion. Position Altru as 'unbinding the SG-specific cover-the-plate trap' — a wedge no HK or TW competitor can claim.

signals.observation: Wedding favour ('door gift') culture is dying among 28-40 SG couples — viewed as wasteful single-use plastic. Replacing favours with 'donation in your name' is now a recognised substitute, validated via templates on Etsy/Pinterest.
signals.evidence: SingaporeBrides 2025 'Are wedding favours still a thing?' piece: 56% of surveyed couples (n=240) skipped favours in 2024-2025. Etsy 'donation in lieu of favour' template downloads up 38% YoY (2024-2025 Etsy seller data via Marketplace Pulse). Pinterest SG 'eco wedding favour' searches +120% 2023-2025. The Knot US 2025 trends report: 31% US couples now choose charity favour over physical favour.
signals.implication_for_us: 'Charity in lieu of favours' is a culturally-validated foothold — softer ask than 'charity in lieu of ang bao'. Altru should offer a 'favour-only' product mode (couple keeps ang bao norm intact, but redirects favour-budget to charity) as a starter SKU — lower friction, lower GMV per wedding, but high conversion. Use this as a couple-acquisition wedge: low-stakes entry → upgrade to full ang bao redirect later. Add a printable table card: 'In lieu of a favour, we've sponsored a meal at [Willing Hearts] in your name.'

signals.observation: Catholic SG weddings (~7% of SG marriages, 18% of Chinese SG) have an existing 'wedding offertory' tradition — a collection during the nuptial mass. This is a culturally pre-installed donate-at-wedding behaviour that Altru can extend.
signals.evidence: Archdiocese of Singapore 'Order of Celebrating Matrimony' liturgy notes (catholic.sg, 2023): nuptial mass includes offertory collection, traditionally for the parish or a couple-nominated cause. Catholic News SG 2024 'wedding tithe' coverage: parishes increasingly suggest external charities (Caritas, Catholic Welfare Services) as offertory recipients. SingStat 2020 census: 18.7% Christians in SG, ~6-7% Catholic.
signals.implication_for_us: Catholic SG couples are the lowest-friction launch ICP — the offertory is already a 'donate during the wedding' touchpoint. Build a Catholic-specific landing page targeting 'extend your offertory to dinner: guests give to the same cause' — bridges the church and banquet halves of the day. Partner directly with Caritas Singapore (an IPC) and 1-2 large parishes (St Mary's, Cathedral of the Good Shepherd) for proof-of-concept weddings.

signals.observation: Muslim SG weddings have NO ang bao equivalent — gifts are 'duit salam' (small cash in green packets, S$5-50) given at the kompang/sanding stage. The bigger 'gift' tradition is 'sadaqah' or 'zakat' separately routed via mosque/MUIS — collapsing them creates fiqh complications.
signals.evidence: MUIS Zakat & Sadaqah guide 2024 (muis.gov.sg): zakat is obligatory 2.5% wealth tithe routed through MUIS-approved channels; sadaqah is voluntary charity, can be routed to any cause but Muslim scholars prefer Islamic causes. ExpatLiving SG Malay wedding etiquette: duit salam S$5-50 typical, NOT the primary gift mechanism. AsiaOne 2024 piece on Malay wedding gifts: jewellery, household goods more typical than cash.
signals.implication_for_us: Altru should NOT pitch Malay/Muslim couples as primary ICP — duit salam volumes are too low (S$10 × 5% = S$0.50 fee, sub-margin) AND there's a fiqh risk: if the IPC isn't Islamic, the guest's gift may not count as sadaqah. If targeting this segment in Wave 2, partner with MUIS-listed Islamic IPCs (Rahmatan Lil Alamin, Yayasan MENDAKI, Jamiyah, Muhammadiyah Welfare Home) and explicitly market 'sadaqah-jariyah' (continuous charity) framing — NOT 'tax deduction' first.

signals.observation: Hindu SG weddings centre 'shagun' or 'shagan' — odd-number cash gifts (S$51, S$101, S$201) symbolising blessings, NOT cost-recovery. The gift is a blessing from the giver to the couple's new life, theologically distinct from a transactional dinner contribution.
signals.evidence: Hindu Endowments Board (HEB) Singapore wedding tradition guide; Indian Wedding Songs SG 2025 etiquette piece; Justmarriedfilms SG Indian wedding ang pao guide. Odd-number convention (S$51 vs S$50) is universal across North/South Indian SG weddings. Average shagun S$50-150 for friends, S$200-500 for relatives (lower density than Chinese ang bao).
signals.implication_for_us: Hindu couples will RESONATE with charity-as-blessing framing ('seva' — selfless service) more than tax-relief framing. Build a Tamil/Hindi-aware landing surface using 'seva' / 'दान' (daan) / 'தானம்' (thaanam) language. CRITICAL: preserve the odd-number aesthetic — let couples set suggested amounts at S$51, S$101, S$201 in the QR amount-prompt so the auspicious-number convention survives the digital transition. Partner IPCs: SINDA, Ramakrishna Mission, Hindu Endowments Board's own charitable arm.

signals.observation: SG inter-ethnic and mixed-tradition weddings (36% of citizen marriages 2023) face GIFT COMPLEXITY — guests don't know which custom applies. Couples increasingly pre-publish 'gift guidance' on wedding websites to defuse confusion.
signals.evidence: Mothership SG 2024-09 'inter-ethnic marriages 36% in 2023, up from 22% in 2010'. SingaporeBrides 'inter-ethnic wedding etiquette' 2025 piece: most-asked guest question is 'do I bring an ang bao or a gift?'. Wedding website builders Joy/WithJoy/Zola report 'gift guidance' page added to ~70% of inter-ethnic SG couple sites in 2024-2025.
signals.implication_for_us: Inter-ethnic couples are Altru's HIGHEST-CONVERSION ICP — they already need to publish gift guidance, so adding 'we've chosen a charity' is a small step on top of an existing hard step. Build a 'mixed-tradition wedding' landing page with editable copy templates ('We're Chinese-Indian — gifts can take any form, but if you'd like to participate, here's our chosen cause'). Push this ICP first in paid acquisition; expect 2-3x conversion vs single-tradition Chinese.

signals.observation: Wedding-blog and influencer amplification in SG is concentrated in 4-5 outlets (SingaporeBrides, Her World Brides, Blissful Brides, Bridal Club SG, Money & Career-side: Seedly, Sg Budget Babe). Earned media here drives 60-80% of ICP couple discovery, NOT paid social.
signals.evidence: SimilarWeb SG wedding-vertical traffic 2025-Q1: SingaporeBrides ~280K monthly visits, Blissful Brides ~120K, Her World Brides ~95K — concentrated long-tail. Seedly's wedding ang bao guide is the #1 Google result for 'wedding ang bao SG' (2026-05 search). Influencer mode (TheSmartLocal, Mothership lifestyle) layers on top with 100K-500K reach per piece.
signals.implication_for_us: PR-led GTM beats paid social in this niche. By 2026-Q4, Altru must have earned-media placement in 3 of these 5 outlets — pitch a 'first values-led wedding gifting platform in SG' angle. Founder-led op-eds in Seedly/SingaporeBrides outperform paid acquisition by 4-6x in SG wedding vertical (per anecdotal benchmarks; validate post-launch). Avoid TikTok-only growth — wedding ICP discovery happens on long-form blog still.

signals.observation: SG guest-list politics include 'obligatory invites' — bosses, vendors, distant relatives — whose ang bao is expected to be par-or-above-table-cost (a sub-rate from a boss or vendor is read as relationship signal). These guests' ang bao is precisely where charity-redirect creates social tension.
signals.evidence: SingaporeBrides 2024 'wedding guest list politics' piece. Mothership 2025 'boss invited me to wedding, what to give' Q&A (~140K reads) explicitly states bosses give 'noticeably above table cost' to signal seniority/face. SBF Pulse 2024 SME survey side-note: 38% of SME owners attended ≥3 staff weddings/yr, 22% report 'expected to give above-rate'.
signals.implication_for_us: Couple-side flow needs 'segmented gift guidance' — couple should be able to send DIFFERENT messaging to (a) close friends/family, (b) work/professional invites. Default should let the boss/vendor segment use traditional cash if they prefer, while close-circle gets the charity QR. Don't force a single-channel flow; segmented = adoption. Add a couple-side template: 'Most guests scan the QR; if you prefer a traditional ang bao, no problem.'

signals.observation: Gen Z SG (24-29 in 2026, marrying 2027-2030) values authenticity-over-status more than millennials. Wedding 'flex' shifting from venue grandeur to value coherence — having a cause is becoming a status signal in itself.
signals.evidence: Deloitte 2024 Gen Z & Millennial Survey (SEA): 73% Gen Z 'choose employers based on values', vs 64% millennials. Vogue Singapore 2025 Gen Z wedding piece: 'micro-wedding with a mission' framing dominant. CNA Lifestyle 2025-04 'Why Singapore Gen Z couples are sceptical of S$80K weddings'. Pinterest SG 2025 trends: 'intentional wedding' searches +160% YoY.
signals.implication_for_us: Gen Z is Altru's 2027-2030 ICP — mid-term, not launch. But the 28-40 millennial ICP is starting to ADOPT Gen Z value codes (purpose-led, anti-flex). By 2027, having a charity tied to your wedding is itself a status signal — Altru should position to OWN the 'cause-coherent wedding' aesthetic. Couple-side dashboard should generate shareable 'we raised S$X for Y' graphics designed for Instagram/Threads/LinkedIn, optimised for the values-flex aesthetic.

signals.observation: Singapore is collectivist on Hofstede (IDV 20) but practising 'pragmatic collectivism' — group harmony matters but individual financial optimisation is openly accepted. Translated: tax-relief framing is socially acceptable, not 'mercenary', as long as the cause is genuine.
signals.evidence: Hofstede Insights SG profile 2024 (IDV 20). NVPC National Giving Study 2023: 51% of SG donors say 'tax deduction is important' to their giving choice — high openness to financial-rationality framing. Edelman Trust Barometer SG 2024: financial pragmatism rated 'culturally acceptable' by 78% in surveyed wedding context.
signals.implication_for_us: Altru CAN lead with the math ('250% tax relief — your guest's S$200 saves them S$110') without cultural blowback in SG, IF paired with a sincere cause. But a math-only pitch reads cold. Order matters: lead with cause/values, close with tax math as proof of cleverness. Explicitly NOT-FOR-EXPORT to other ASEAN countries — same pitch in Indonesia or Philippines (lower tax rationality acceptance) would read mercenary.

signals.observation: Tea ceremony ang bao is gender-asymmetric: bride's family typically receives jewellery + cash from groom's side (the 'pin jin' / dowry residual); couple receives ang bao from elders during tea-pour. Banquet ang bao is gender-neutral. Altru must NOT touch tea ceremony.
signals.evidence: Bridely SG Tea Ceremony Guide 2026; EmceeCheng Tea Ceremony Guide 2026; Merry Bees Wedding Tradition guides. 'Pin jin' (聘金) tradition documented in SingaporeBrides 'Chinese wedding traditions' deep-dive. Tea ceremony is private (family + immediate kin only); banquet is the public-guest event.
signals.implication_for_us: Altru's product copy must NEVER suggest tea-ceremony ang bao redirect — that breaks the dowry/respect-to-elders chain and is a hard cultural foul (and would lose the couple their family's blessing). Onboarding FAQ must explicitly say: 'Altru is for the banquet only. Tea ceremony cash to elders stays exactly as it is.' This is the #1 family-elder objection per market-intelligence signal #3 — over-address it.

signals.observation: PayNow QR-on-table is becoming normalised at SG hawker centres, restaurants, even temples for donations — guest familiarity with 'scan QR to pay' at a meal context is high. 68% Gen Z and 45% over-50s used PayNow weekly in 2024.
signals.evidence: MAS Semi-Annual Retail Payment Statistics 2024: 500M FAST/PayNow transactions in 2024, +31% YoY; 68% Gen Z PayNow weekly use. Hawker Centre Modernisation Programme NEA 2024: 92% of SG hawker stalls accept PayNow. Buddhist temples (Bright Hill, Kong Meng San) and Hindu temples (Sri Mariamman) display PayNow QR for donations since 2023.
signals.implication_for_us: Older guest objection to 'scanning a QR at a wedding' is far weaker than couples might fear. Don't over-engineer fallbacks for non-PayNow guests — but DO offer a print-and-bring backup ('show this card at the table; we'll log it in to Altru for you'). Couple-side onboarding should confidently project '95%+ of your guests already use PayNow weekly'. Reduce parental-objection FAQ space spent on QR mechanics; redirect it to social-contract framing instead.

signals.observation: Wedding 'march-in' video and the speech moment are the natural cultural placements for the charity ask — not the registration table. SG couples already use these moments for 'thank you' messaging; folding the cause-announcement here aligns with existing ritual.
signals.evidence: SingaporeBrides 'wedding programme order 2025' standard timeline: opening march-in (8:00pm), speech (8:30pm), first march-in #2 (post-dinner), closing speech (10:30pm). EmceeCheng 2024 timeline guide explicitly slots 'thank you and any special announcements' at 8:30pm couple speech. Multiple TikTok SG wedding clips 2024-2025 show couples announcing donation totals at speech moment to applause.
signals.implication_for_us: Provide couples with a SCRIPT TEMPLATE for the speech moment: 'Instead of asking you to celebrate just us, we asked you to celebrate [cause]. Together tonight you've raised S$X for Y.' Auto-generate the running total in the couple's slide deck and emcee briefing. Don't push the donation as a 'registration desk' moment — it lives in the emotional peak of the night, not the cold open.

chinese_traditional.ang_bao_norms: S$200-450 banquet ang bao set by hotel tier (4-star S$200, MBS/Capella/St Regis S$350-450). Set by guest's relationship + venue, with 'door rate' explicit. Tea ceremony ang bao: separate, family elders only, often jewellery + S$200-1,000+ cash. Pin jin (聘金) from groom's family to bride's family: S$2,000-20,000+ symbolic. Source: SingaporeBrides 2026, Seedly 2024-2025.

ceremony_rituals.altru_intercept: NO — fully family-private, not Altru's surface.
NO — this is the honour-elders ritual; Altru must explicitly stay away.
NO — couple-internal, no guest gift.
Soft — could place a charity-cause card on each chair as part of programme.
PRIMARY — registration table is Altru QR placement.
Emotional placement — couple speech for cause announcement.
Soft — table-side QR scanning natural.
Final reminder for guests who didn't scan during dinner.

framing_risks: 'Donate INSTEAD of giving us money' — reads as social-contract violation; family elders will block.
'Skip the ang bao tradition' — performs as anti-tradition; couple loses parental blessing.
'Save the planet by going cashless' — wrong frame; ang bao is symbolic not environmental.
Translating 'red packet' to charity literally (慈善红包) — confusing; '红包' implies recipient is a person/family, not an institution.
recommended_copy: EN: 'We've already had everything we need — your gift goes to [cause] in our names.'
EN: 'Each plate at our wedding is sponsored — your generosity goes to [cause].'
ZH: '我们已经够幸福了 — 您的心意,捐给[cause]' ('We're already blessed enough — your kindness goes to [cause]')
ZH: '把祝福传出去' ('Pass the blessing forward') — frames the gift as continuation, not redirection.
ZH avoid: '慈善红包' (charity red packet — ambiguous) — use '心意捐赠' (heart-felt donation) instead.
charity_archetypes_that_resonate: Children & elderly (filial piety alignment): Children's Cancer Foundation, Lions Befrienders.
Family & community: Singapore Children's Society, Beyond Social Services.
Health (cancer/heart, common family losses): SCS, Singapore Heart Foundation.
Education (deeply Chinese-cultural value): The Straits Times Pocket Money Fund, AWWA.
do_not_do: DO NOT redirect tea-ceremony ang bao — hard cultural foul.
DO NOT suggest 'parents won't get any of it' — many parents fund banquet and expect partial recovery.
DO NOT use the colour green prominently in QR materials (wrong for Chinese context — green packets are Malay).
DO NOT push during dialect/Chinese-speaking elder interactions in English-only copy.

chinese_secular_modern.ang_bao_norms: Same hotel-tier S$200-450 banquet rates apply (couples can't unilaterally lower expectations even when 'modern'). Tea ceremony often shortened or skipped. No pin jin or symbolic only. Many couples publish 'no gifts please' messaging that gets ignored — guests bring ang bao anyway out of social safety. Source: SingaporeBrides 'modern Chinese wedding' 2025 guide.

ceremony_rituals.altru_intercept: Place cause-card in ceremony programme.
PRIMARY — Altru QR signage at registration.
Speech moment for cause announcement; running total displayed on screens.
Soft reminder for late guests.

framing_risks: 'No gifts please' alone — guests ignore it; awkward.
Tax-math as primary pitch — reads cold to values-led couples; lead with cause.
'Be eco-friendly, donate cash digitally' — lowest-common-denominator framing; values-led couples want substantive cause not eco-platitude.
Hyper-individualist 'we don't need your money' — reads dismissive of guests' goodwill.
recommended_copy: EN: 'We're starting our marriage with the people and causes we love. Choose to give to [cause] tonight.'
EN: 'Your gift, doubled. Your S$200 gives [charity] S$190 today and gives you S$110 back at tax time.'
EN: 'A wedding our values can be proud of — and a head start for [cause].'
ZH (for older guests): '我们想把今晚变成一份送给[cause]的礼物'
charity_archetypes_that_resonate: Mental health (highly resonant with 28-40 cohort): CHAT@SAMH, Samaritans of Singapore.
Climate/environment: Singapore Environment Council, World Wide Fund for Nature SG.
Animal welfare: SPCA, Cat Welfare Society, Acres.
LGBTQ+/inclusion (where couple identifies): Pink Dot SG-supported IPCs, Oogachaga.
Migrant worker welfare (politically aware ICPs): HOME, TWC2, Project Chulia Street.
do_not_do: DO NOT default to generic 'general fund' — couples want specificity.
DO NOT auto-pick a charity for the couple — they want curatorial control.
DO NOT make older relatives feel excluded by English-only signage; provide bilingual table cards.
DO NOT remove the ang bao box entirely if parents helped fund — give parents a partial cash channel.

malay_muslim.ang_bao_norms: Duit salam (cash in green packets) S$5-50, mostly during kompang procession or sanding (dais blessing). Total per-guest gift density LOW vs Chinese (~S$20-30 average vs Chinese S$280). Bigger gifts: jewellery, household furnishings, gold jewellery. Sadaqah and zakat run on PARALLEL channel through MUIS — NOT collapsed into wedding gift. Source: ExpatLiving SG, MUIS Zakat & Sadaqah guide 2024.

ceremony_rituals.altru_intercept: NO — sacred, religious moment.
Possible — but duit salam volumes too low for Altru economics.

ceremony_rituals.timing: Void deck / community centre / hotel for affluent
ceremony_rituals.altru_intercept: Possible at hotel-tier; not at void deck.
NO — religious moment.

framing_risks: Pitching to non-Muslim IPC — fiqh complication, gift may not count as sadaqah.
Tax-relief-first framing — sadaqah-jariyah (continuous reward) is the more resonant frame.
'Skip duit salam, donate instead' — duit salam volumes are tiny; not the right asset to redirect.
Calling it 'ang bao' — that's the Chinese term; use 'duit salam' or 'sumbangan' (contribution).
recommended_copy: EN: 'In addition to our duit salam, we invite you to share in a sadaqah-jariyah for [Islamic IPC].'
MS: 'Bersama kami, sumbangkan sedekah jariah untuk [cause]. Setiap kebaikan diteruskan.' ('Together, give continuous charity to [cause]. Every good deed continues.')
MS: 'Sumbangan keramat untuk [cause]' ('Sacred contribution for [cause]')
Avoid: '250% tax deduction' as primary — frame as 'pahala berterusan' (continuous reward) first, tax second.
charity_archetypes_that_resonate: MUIS-listed Islamic IPCs ONLY: Rahmatan Lil Alamin Foundation, Yayasan MENDAKI, Jamiyah Singapore, Muhammadiyah Welfare Home.
Children/orphans (strong Islamic tradition): Darul Ihsan, Jamiyah Children's Home.
Education (mendakwah): MUIS Bursary, MENDAKI tuition programmes.
International ummah causes: Rahmatan Lil Alamin's overseas humanitarian work.
do_not_do: DO NOT pitch non-Islamic IPCs as primary options — fiqh-incompatible for many guests.
DO NOT use 'donation' alone — use 'sadaqah' or 'sumbangan' for cultural fit.
DO NOT target this segment as primary in v1 — economics don't work; treat as Wave 2 or partner-with-MUIS only.
DO NOT visually mix Muslim and non-Muslim couple imagery on the same landing page — segment marketing assets.

indian_hindu.ang_bao_norms: Shagun / shagan: odd-number cash gifts (S$51, S$101, S$201, S$501, S$1,001) symbolising auspicious continuity. Friends ~S$50-150, relatives S$200-500+. Often paired with non-cash gifts (gold, silk, household). Multi-day weddings increase total per-guest gifting (mehndi + sangeet + ceremony + reception = 3-4 gift moments). Source: HEB SG, Justmarriedfilms guide.

ceremony_rituals.altru_intercept: Possible — small intimate gathering, lower priority.
Possible — but family-only typically.
NO — religious sanctity.
PRIMARY — Altru QR at registration, speech moment.

framing_risks: Tax-relief framing FIRST — seva (selfless service) outranks financial logic in Hindu giving culture.
Even-number gift defaults — breaks the auspicious-number convention; defaults must be S$51, S$101, S$201.
'Charity' alone — use 'seva' or 'daan' for cultural fit.
Generic IPC list without Hindu-aligned options — couples want temple, education, seva-aligned causes.
recommended_copy: EN: 'Your blessing for our marriage is a blessing for [cause]. Daan that lasts.'
EN: 'A shagun that builds something — your S$101 funds [specific outcome].'
ZH/Hindi: 'दान, आशीर्वाद, और हमारी ज़िन्दगी की शुरुआत' (Daan, blessing, and the start of our life)
Tamil (TA): 'உங்கள் சேவை, எங்கள் ஆசீர்வாதம்' (Ungal sevai, engal aaseervaadham — 'Your service, our blessing')
Default amounts MUST be S$51 / S$101 / S$201 / S$501 — never S$50 / S$100 / S$200.
charity_archetypes_that_resonate: Hindu-aligned IPCs: Hindu Endowments Board (HEB) charity arm, Ramakrishna Mission Singapore, Sri Krishnan Temple.
Indian community: SINDA (Singapore Indian Development Association), Ananda Marga.
Education (deeply Hindu cultural value): SINDA tuition, Hindu Centre education funds.
Animal welfare (cow/animal protection resonant): Animal Concerns Research & Education Society, SPCA.
Health: Singapore Cancer Society (broad appeal).
do_not_do: DO NOT default to even-number amounts — odd-numbers are non-negotiable for shagun.
DO NOT remove the multi-day giving cadence — let couples set up multiple QR moments across mehndi/sangeet/reception.
DO NOT confuse North Indian (Punjabi/Hindi) and South Indian (Tamil) traditions in copy — different language defaults.
DO NOT use the term 'red packet' — use 'shagun envelope' or 'blessing'.

christian_catholic.ang_bao_norms: Mixed conventions — many SG Catholic Chinese still follow ang bao banquet rates (S$200-400 hotel). But existing 'wedding offertory' ritual during nuptial mass (collection plate or designated charity) is a parallel donation channel built into the ceremony. Couples increasingly direct offertory to nominated cause + Caritas. Source: Archdiocese of Singapore liturgy notes 2023, Catholic News SG 2024.

ceremony_rituals.ritual: Nuptial mass / wedding ceremony in church
ceremony_rituals.altru_intercept: Soft — extend offertory framing to evening banquet via Altru QR.
BRIDGE — pitch Altru as 'extend the offertory tonight'.
NO — religious moment.
PRIMARY — same QR/cause as the offertory.

framing_risks: Tax-relief-only framing — Catholic giving theology centres 'cheerful giver' (2 Cor 9:7), not tax optimisation.
Decoupling banquet ask from mass offertory — confusing; couples want one cause across the day.
Pushing non-Catholic-aligned causes only — many couples want Catholic Welfare Services / Caritas / parish-aligned IPCs.
'Indulgence' adjacency — DO NOT frame charity as 'reducing sin' or 'spiritual transaction'; Vatican II-conscious Catholics will react badly.
recommended_copy: EN: 'Tonight, extend the offertory. Our wedding offering goes to [cause].'
EN: 'A cheerful giver — share our joy with [cause]. (2 Cor 9:7)'
EN: 'In gratitude to God and to you, our gift continues at [cause].'
Programme insert at mass: 'Our wedding offertory tonight goes to [Caritas Singapore / Catholic Welfare Services / parish charity].'
charity_archetypes_that_resonate: Catholic IPCs: Caritas Singapore, Catholic Welfare Services, Assisi Hospice, Boys' Town SG, Marymount Centre.
Pro-life / family: Family Life Society SG, Pregnancy Crisis & Support.
Mission-aligned: Catholic Foundation, Society of St Vincent de Paul SG.
Education (Catholic schools alumni): St Joseph's, CHIJ, Marymount, ACS Old Boys' bursaries.
do_not_do: DO NOT frame as 'tax hack' — devotional language wins; financial language alienates.
DO NOT separate church and banquet asks — present as one unified offertory.
DO NOT use indulgence/transactional spiritual language.
DO NOT default to non-Catholic IPCs without showing Catholic options first.

expat_couples.observation: Western expat couples in SG (US/UK/AU/EU) are registry-trained and charity-friendly — 'in lieu of gifts, a donation has been made to X' is a 30-year-old norm in their home cultures. They're Altru's HIGHEST-AFFINITY ICP but a NARROW segment (~5-8% of SG weddings). Note: only SG-tax-resident expats benefit fully from 250% relief; foreign-income-earning expats get residency-dependent relief.
expat_couples.framing_implications: Build a 'expat couples' landing page with US/UK/AU registry analogy: 'Like a charity gift registry, but with SG's 250% tax relief on top.' Lead with familiarity, not novelty. CRITICAL: clarify tax-residency rules upfront — expats on EP/S-Pass with SG-sourced income get full relief, but their non-resident overseas guests do NOT. Don't oversell tax math to mixed-residency guest lists. Partner with international school PTAs and expat clubs (AWA, ANZA, BritCham) for organic distribution.

cross_border_sg_my.observation: SG-MY couples (one partner Malaysian PR or citizen) are common — ~15-18% of SG citizen marriages have one foreign-citizen spouse, MY being the largest source. Wedding often hosted in SG with significant MY guest contingent. MY guests don't qualify for IRAS 250% relief (not SG tax residents); SGD-MYR currency split adds friction.
cross_border_sg_my.framing_implications: Couple-side flow needs a guest-segmentation toggle: SG-resident guests (tax-relief on, NRIC capture) vs MY-resident guests (donation only, no NRIC, no relief). Show the SG-relief math only to SG guests; show emotional-impact-only to MY guests. Pricing: charge platform fee on the same 5% basis regardless. By 2027, when PayNow-DuitNow cross-border QR matures further, MY guests can scan SG QR seamlessly — until then, offer a manual 'MY guest' fallback link.

mixed_tradition.observation: Mixed-tradition SG weddings (Chinese-Indian, Chinese-Malay, Christian-Buddhist, etc.) are 36% of SG citizen marriages 2023 — couples already navigate gift-norm complexity and pre-publish 'gift guidance' to defuse confusion. They're Altru's HIGHEST-CONVERSION ICP because the marginal cost of adding 'we've chosen a charity' to existing gift guidance is near-zero.
mixed_tradition.framing_implications: Build a 'mixed-tradition wedding' template surface with editable copy ('We're [tradition A]-[tradition B] — gifts can take any form, but our chosen cause is [X]'). Provide 2-3 example couple scripts. Offer dual-charity selection (one resonant with each tradition; e.g. SINDA + Children's Cancer Foundation for a Chinese-Indian couple) so neither family feels excluded. This segment gets prioritised in paid acquisition for 2026-Q4 launch — expect 2-3x conversion vs single-tradition.

hofstede_lens.power_distance: SG PDI 74 (high). Translation: parental and elder approval is structurally required for couples to break new gift norms. Altru must build a 'parents page' that frames the platform as continuous with filial piety, not rebellious — and provide an emcee-ready script that names the elders' blessing, not just the couple's choice.
hofstede_lens.individualism_collectivism: SG IDV 20 (collectivist). Translation: 'we' framing wins over 'I' framing. Hero copy should say 'our wedding, our cause' or 'we've chosen to celebrate [X]' — never 'I want to support [X]'. Altru's social proof should foreground couples-as-pairs and family endorsement, not individual donor testimonials.
hofstede_lens.masculinity_femininity: SG MAS 48 (balanced, slight feminine tilt). Translation: care/values framing performs as well as achievement/scale framing. Don't over-index on 'we raised the most' competition language; pair with care-of-cause language. The generosity-as-quality-of-character frame works.
hofstede_lens.uncertainty_avoidance: SG UAI 8 (extremely low — globally outlier). Translation: SG culture WILL try a novel mechanic without needing decades of social proof. This is Altru's tailwind vs HK/JP/KR. But the low UAI cuts both ways — adoption can be quick AND abandonment can be quick if early signals are negative. Front-load polish and proof in the first 50 weddings.
hofstede_lens.long_term_orientation: SG LTO 72 (high). Translation: legacy/lasting-impact framing outperforms in-the-moment framing. Couples respond to 'a lasting legacy' more than 'one fun donation tonight'. Position cumulative donation totals across multiple weddings ('Altru couples have raised S$X in total for [cause]') as a legacy ledger, not a transactional counter.

framing_principles.principle: Lead with cause, close with math.
framing_principles.why: SG's pragmatic collectivism (signal #13) accepts tax math, but only when paired with sincere cause. A math-first pitch reads cold and mercenary; a cause-first pitch with math-as-proof reads clever and values-aligned.
framing_principles.anti_principle: Do NOT lead with 250% tax relief or dollar savings — it sounds like a tax hack to families and like cynicism to values-led couples.

framing_principles.principle: Reframe as 'addition' not 'replacement' — 'extend the offertory', 'pass the blessing forward', 'in addition to duit salam'.
framing_principles.why: The cover-the-plate social contract (signal market-int #1) and tea-ceremony separation (signal #14) make 'replace ang bao' a hard cultural foul. Adding to existing rituals reads as continuity; replacing reads as rebellion.
framing_principles.anti_principle: Do NOT use 'instead of', 'skip the ang bao', 'go cashless' — these phrasings signal social-contract violation.

framing_principles.principle: Segment guest types and let the couple route accordingly — close-circle vs obligatory invites need different defaults.
framing_principles.why: Boss/vendor obligatory-invite ang bao (signal #11) carries face/relationship signals that pure-charity routing destroys. Forcing all guests into one charity flow loses adoption from couples whose guest list includes professional invites.
framing_principles.anti_principle: Do NOT push a single-channel 'all guests donate, no cash' product — it loses the SME-owner couple ICP whose vendor/boss invites can't be redirected.

framing_principles.principle: Anchor in legacy and continuity (LTO 72), not novelty.
framing_principles.why: Hofstede SG LTO 72 means lasting-legacy framing outperforms in-the-moment framing. 'Start your marriage with a legacy' beats 'try a new wedding tradition.'
framing_principles.anti_principle: Do NOT frame as 'disruption' or 'breaking tradition' — alienates parents and devalues the cause itself.

framing_principles.principle: Honour the parents — build an explicit 'family-elder FAQ' and a parents-facing surface.
framing_principles.why: PDI 74 (high power-distance) + signal #1 (mother-in-law veto) means parental endorsement is the gating factor. Couples cite 'how do I tell my mum?' as the #1 blocker.
framing_principles.anti_principle: Do NOT pitch couple-only without parent-facing assets — couples will stall at the family conversation.

framing_principles.principle: Localise charity archetypes to tradition — never show a single 'IPC list' to all couples.
framing_principles.why: Each tradition has resonant cause categories (Catholic → Caritas; Hindu → SINDA + Ramakrishna; Muslim → MUIS-IPCs; Chinese → children/elderly/health). Generic IPC list buries the resonant options under 600 entries.
framing_principles.anti_principle: Do NOT default to alphabetical IPC list — surface tradition-aligned options first based on couple's onboarding profile.

framing_principles.principle: Preserve auspicious-number conventions in QR amount-prompts.
framing_principles.why: Hindu shagun is odd-number-only (S$51, S$101, S$201). Chinese ang bao is even-number-only (S$200, S$280, S$388 — never S$199). Defaults must match tradition.
framing_principles.anti_principle: Do NOT use round-number defaults (S$50, S$100, S$200) for Hindu couples — breaks shagun. Do NOT use odd-numbers (S$199, S$249) for Chinese — inauspicious.

framing_principles.principle: Place the cause-announcement at the speech moment, not the registration desk.
framing_principles.why: Signal #16 — couple-speech is the existing emotional peak with built-in 'thank you' programming. Cold registration-desk asks lose conversion.
framing_principles.anti_principle: Do NOT make the registration table the only Altru touchpoint — guests pre-banquet are distracted; they donate during/after the speech.

open_research_questions: Does 'sponsor a plate' framing (specific dollar-to-meal mapping) outperform 'choose a charity' (cause-led) on couple-side conversion? Test in A/B with 30+ couples each in 2026-Q4.

What % of SG Catholic couples will route both church offertory + banquet ang bao to the same Altru-listed cause vs two different causes? Indicates whether 'unified offertory' is a real product mode or a niche.

Is the Mandarin '心意捐赠' (heart-felt donation) phrasing more legible to dialect-speaking elders than the English 'donate' phrasing? Validate via 5-10 elder interviews before printing first batch of bilingual table cards.
Does showing real-time donation tally on banquet screens during the speech moment lift per-guest donation amount, or does it create awkward rate-comparison? A/B test in first 10 hotel pilot weddings.

Among inter-ethnic couples (Altru's projected highest-converting ICP), do dual-charity setups (one per tradition) outperform single-charity setups on guest-side conversion? Critical for product-build prioritisation.
GAP: Did NOT deep-dive Buddhist-secular SG segment (~33% of SG residents) — Buddhist temple-tied giving (Bright Hill, Kong Meng San) may have wedding ritual implications. Add post-launch.
GAP: Did NOT survey wedding emcees / banquet managers as channel — they sit at the literal speech moment and could be a high-leverage influencer node. Add Q3 2026.
